evidence_quotes.quote: pressure other platforms into removing content that does not violate the platform's terms of service

paragraphs.text: I am rather concerned about the fact that large monopolies in the online space, such as credit card companies, are capable of enforcing what is and isn't allowed to be displayed on online market platforms, that these companies do not hold ownership over, due to the influence that they hold. This effectively allows these monopolies to pressure other platforms into removing content that does not violate the platform's terms of service, simply because the monopolies do not like said content. I believe doing so is anti competitive and puts both consumers and online traders at risk of having their rights violated, while also contributing to an uneven playing field for online traders, where a few powerful companies can determine what can and cannot be sold.
